---
title: "Cuisines MCA Analysis"
author: "Omar Lizardo"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

# Setup
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
    knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
    library(conflicted)
    library(here)
    library(dplyr)
    library(tidyr)
    library(ggplot2)
    library(sjPlot)
    library(haven)
    library(patchwork)
    library(factoextra)
    library(FactoMineR)
    library(ggrepel)
    library(stringr)
    library(corrplot)
    library(MASS)
    library(nnet)
    library(ggeffects)
    library(knitr)
    
    conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)
    conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select)
    conflicts_prefer(dplyr::summarize)
```

# Getting and recoding data
```{r Getting and Recoding Data}
    source(here("recode.dat.R"))
    dat <- recode.dat() |> 
        dplyr::select(
            # dine_out (2 variables)
            fancy_restaurant, fast_food,
            # food_qual (6 variables)
            light, rich, authentic, familiar, big, small,
            # food (6 variables)
            caviar, oysters, nuggets, cheeseburger, sourdough, avocado,
            # authenticity (15 variables)
            japanese:lebanese,
            # quant.sup (7 variables)
            age, income, educ, peduc, arts, city, relig,
            # quali.sup (12 variables)
            race.f, sex.f, gend.f, spol.f, epol.f, educ.f, arts.f, inc.f, age.f, peduc.f, city.f, relig.f
        ) |> 
        na.omit()
    names(dat)
```

# Principal Component Analysis
```{r Principal Component Analysis}
    mfa.res <- PCA(dat, 
        graph = FALSE, 
        scale.unit = TRUE, 
        quanti.sup = 30:36, 
        quali.sup = 37:48
        )
    mfa.dat <- dat |> cbind(mfa.res$ind$coord)
```

# PCA scree plot & coordinates
```{r Scree Plot}
    p_eig <- fviz_eig(mfa.res, choice = "eigenvalue", ncp = 10) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 2, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
        ggtitle("PCA Scree Plot")
    ggsave(here("Plots", "eig.png"), p_eig, width = 8, height = 6)
```

# Correlation matrix heatmap
```{r Correlation Heatmap}
    plot.mat <- get_pca_var(mfa.res)$cor
    plot.mat_df <- plot.mat |> 
        as.data.frame() |> 
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "Variable") |> 
        pivot_longer(
            cols = starts_with("Dim"),
            names_to = "Dimension",
            values_to = "Correlation"
        ) |> 
        filter(Dimension %in% c("Dim.1", "Dim.2", "Dim.3", "Dim.4", "Dim.5")) |> 
        mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = rownames(plot.mat)))
    
    # Split variables into two parts: practices/flavors (up to avocado) and the 15 cuisines (starting with japanese)
    vars <- levels(plot.mat_df$Variable)
    idx_avocado <- which(vars == "avocado")
    vars1 <- vars[1:idx_avocado]
    vars2 <- vars[(idx_avocado + 1):length(vars)]

    plot_corr_subset <- function(subset_vars, filename) {
        df_subset <- plot.mat_df |> filter(Variable %in% subset_vars)
        max_corr <- max(abs(plot.mat_df$Correlation))
        corr_limits <- c(-max_corr, max_corr)
        
        p <- ggplot(df_subset, aes(y = Variable, x = Dimension, fill = Correlation)) + 
            geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.5, linetype = 1) + coord_fixed() +
            scale_fill_gradient2(
                        low = "#FF0000",
                        mid = "#FFFFCC",
                        high = "#075AFF",
                        midpoint = 0, limits = corr_limits) +
            geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Correlation)), 
                        vjust = 0.5, 
                        fontface = "bold", 
                        color = "black", size = 4.5) +
            scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
            scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
            theme_minimal() +
            theme(
                axis.title = element_blank(),
                text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
                legend.position = "right",
                legend.title = element_blank(),
                panel.grid = element_blank()
            ) 
        ggsave(here("Plots", filename), p, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    }

    plot_corr_subset(vars1, "varcor_1.png")
    plot_corr_subset(vars2, "varcor_2.png")
```

# Multivariate Analysis of Variance (MANOVA)
```{r MANOVA}
    # Fit MANOVA model with comprehensive demographic, ideological, and interaction terms
    manova_model <- manova(
        cbind(Dim.1, Dim.2, Dim.3) ~ race.f + age.f + educ.f + peduc.f + city.f + arts.f + spol.f + epol.f + relig.f + inc.f + gend.f + sex.f + 
            educ.f:spol.f + gend.f:sex.f, 
        data = mfa.dat
    )
    
    # Save MANOVA table as HTML (Beautiful custom CSS table)
    sum_manova <- summary(manova_model, test = "Pillai")
    stats_df <- as.data.frame(sum_manova$stats)
    
    clean_labels <- c(
      "race.f" = "Ethnoracial ID",
      "age.f" = "Age / Generation",
      "educ.f" = "Education Level",
      "peduc.f" = "Parent's Education",
      "city.f" = "City Size (Urbanicity)",
      "arts.f" = "Childhood Arts Exposure",
      "spol.f" = "Pol. Ideology (Social)",
      "epol.f" = "Pol. Ideology (Econ.)",
      "relig.f" = "Religiosity Service Freq.",
      "inc.f" = "Household Income",
      "gend.f" = "Gender ID",
      "sex.f" = "Sexual ID",
      "educ.f:spol.f" = "Education x Social Ideology Interaction",
      "gend.f:sex.f" = "Gender x Sexuality Interaction",
      "Residuals" = "Residuals"
    )
    rownames(stats_df) <- clean_labels[rownames(stats_df)]

    html_content <- paste0(
      "<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n",
      "<meta charset='utf-8'>\n",
      "<style>\n",
      "  body { font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; background-color: #fdfefe; padding: 20px; }\n",
      "  table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; max-width: 950px; margin: 20px auto; background-color: #ffffff; box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; }\n",
      "  th { background-color: #2c3e50; color: #ffffff; padding: 14px 18px; text-align: left; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; border-bottom: 3px solid #1abc9c; }\n",
      "  td { padding: 12px 18px; border-bottom: 1px solid #ecf0f1; font-size: 14px; color: #2c3e50; }\n",
      "  tr:nth-child(even) { background-color: #f8f9fa; }\n",
      "  tr:hover { background-color: #e8f8f5; transition: background-color 0.2s ease; }\n",
      "  .caption { font-size: 18px; font-weight: bold; color: #2c3e50; margin: 15px auto; text-align: center; max-width: 950px; border-left: 4px solid #1abc9c; padding-left: 10px; }\n",
      "  .sig { font-weight: bold; color: #27ae60; }\n",
      "  .ns { color: #95a5a6; font-style: italic; }\n",
      "  .notes { font-size: 12px; color: #7f8c8d; margin: 10px auto; text-align: center; max-width: 950px; }\n",
      "</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n",
      "<div class='caption'>Table 1. Multivariate Analysis of Variance (MANOVA) Results with Interactions</div>\n",
      "<table>\n",
      "  <thead>\n",
      "    <tr>\n",
      "      <th>Socio-Demographic Predictor</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Df</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Pillai's Trace</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Approx. F</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Num Df</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Den Df</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Pr(&gt;F)</th>\n",
      "      <th style='text-align: center;'>Significance</th>\n",
      "    </tr>\n",
      "  </thead>\n",
      "  <tbody>\n"
    )

    for (i in 1:nrow(stats_df)) {
      row_name <- rownames(stats_df)[i]
      row <- stats_df[i, ]
      
      df_val <- row[["Df"]]
      pillai <- row[["Pillai"]]
      approx_f <- row[["approx F"]]
      num_df <- row[["num Df"]]
      den_df <- row[["den Df"]]
      p_val <- row[["Pr(>F)"]]
      
      pillai_str <- if (is.na(pillai)) "" else sprintf("%.5f", pillai)
      f_str <- if (is.na(approx_f)) "" else sprintf("%.3f", approx_f)
      num_df_str <- if (is.na(num_df)) "" else as.character(num_df)
      den_df_str <- if (is.na(den_df)) "" else as.character(den_df)
      
      p_str <- ""
      sig_class <- ""
      stars <- ""
      if (!is.na(p_val)) {
        p_str <- if (p_val < 0.0001) "&lt; 0.0001" else sprintf("%.5f", p_val)
        if (p_val < 0.001) {
          stars <- "***"
          sig_class <- "class='sig'"
        } else if (p_val < 0.01) {
          stars <- "**"
          sig_class <- "class='sig'"
        } else if (p_val < 0.05) {
          stars <- "*"
          sig_class <- "class='sig'"
        } else if (p_val < 0.1) {
          stars <- "."
          sig_class <- "class='ns'"
        } else {
          stars <- "n.s."
          sig_class <- "class='ns'"
        }
      }
      
      html_content <- paste0(
        html_content,
        "    <tr>\n",
        "      <td style='font-weight: bold; color: #34495e;'>", row_name, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;'>", df_val, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;'>", pillai_str, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;'>", f_str, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;'>", num_df_str, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;'>", den_df_str, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center; font-weight: 500;'>", p_str, "</td>\n",
        "      <td style='text-align: center;' ", sig_class, ">", stars, "</td>\n",
        "    </tr>\n"
      )
    }

    html_content <- paste0(
      html_content,
      "  </tbody>\n",
      "</table>\n",
      "<div class='notes'>Significance codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 (n.s. = not significant)</div>\n",
      "</body>\n</html>"
    )

    writeLines(html_content, here("Tabs", "manova_table.html"))
    
    # Post-hoc pairwise comparisons for race/ethnicity
    levels_race <- levels(mfa.dat$race.f)
    pairs <- combn(levels_race, 2)
    results <- list()
    for (i in 1:ncol(pairs)) {
      g1 <- pairs[1, i]
      g2 <- pairs[2, i]
      sub_dat <- mfa.dat |> dplyr::filter(race.f %in% c(g1, g2))
      fit <- manova(cbind(Dim.1, Dim.2, Dim.3) ~ race.f, data = sub_dat)
      results[[i]] <- data.frame(
        Comparison = paste(g1, "vs.", g2),
        Pillai = summary(fit, test = "Pillai")$stats[1, "Pillai"],
        p_value = summary(fit, test = "Pillai")$stats[1, "Pr(>F)"]
      )
    }
    pairwise_df <- do.call(rbind, results) |> 
      mutate(p_adj_bonferroni = p.adjust(p_value, method = "bonferroni")) |> 
      arrange(p_value)
    
    writeLines(
        knitr::kable(pairwise_df, format = "html", caption = "Table 2. Post-hoc Pairwise MANOVA Comparisons for Race/Ethnicity Groups"), 
        here("Tabs", "pairwise_manovas.html")
    )
    
    # Univariate Tukey HSD on Dimension 1
    tukey_d1 <- TukeyHSD(aov(Dim.1 ~ race.f, data = mfa.dat))
    writeLines(
        knitr::kable(as.data.frame(tukey_d1$race.f), format = "html", caption = "Table 3. Tukey's Honestly Significant Difference (HSD) pairwise results on Dimension 1 (Fancy Taste) for Race/Ethnicity"), 
        here("Tabs", "tukey_hsd_dim1.html")
    )
```

## Post-hoc interaction plots
```{r Interaction Plots}
    # Plot Dimension 1 Sexuality x Social Ideology Interaction with Confidence Intervals
    pred_dim1 <- ggpredict(lm(Dim.1 ~ sex.f * spol.f, data = mfa.dat), terms = c("spol.f", "sex.f"))
    p_int1 <- ggplot(as.data.frame(pred_dim1), aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, group = group)) +
        geom_line(linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(0.2)) +
        geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), size = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.2)) +
        labs(
            title = "Interaction of Sexuality & Social Ideology on Dimension 1 (Fancy Taste)",
            x = "Social Ideology",
            y = "Predicted Score (MFA Dim 1)",
            color = "Sexuality"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    ggsave(here("Plots", "interaction_dim1.png"), p_int1, width = 8, height = 6)

    # Plot Dimension 2 Sexuality x Social Ideology Interaction with Confidence Intervals
    pred_dim2 <- ggpredict(lm(Dim.2 ~ sex.f * spol.f, data = mfa.dat), terms = c("spol.f", "sex.f"))
    p_int2 <- ggplot(as.data.frame(pred_dim2), aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, group = group)) +
        geom_line(linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(0.2)) +
        geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), size = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.2)) +
        labs(
            title = "Interaction of Sexuality & Social Ideology on Dimension 2 (Common Taste)",
            x = "Social Ideology",
            y = "Predicted Score (MFA Dim 2)",
            color = "Sexuality"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    ggsave(here("Plots", "interaction_dim2.png"), p_int2, width = 8, height = 6)
```

# Hierarchical Clustering on Principal Components (HCPC)
```{r HCPC}
    # Run HCPC clustering (using 3 clusters)
    hcpc_res <- HCPC(mfa.res, nb.clust = 3, graph = FALSE)
    mfa.dat$Cluster <- hcpc_res$data.clust$clust
    
    # Intuitive labels for clusters
    cluster_labels <- c(
        "1" = "Everyday Convenience Seekers",
        "2" = "Traditional/Conservative Homebody",
        "3" = "Culinary Explorer/High-End Enthusiast"
    )
    mfa.dat$ClusterName <- factor(cluster_labels[as.character(mfa.dat$Cluster)], levels = cluster_labels)
    
    # Calculate centroids for plotting
    centroids_plot <- mfa.dat |> 
        group_by(ClusterName) |> 
        summarize(Dim.1 = mean(Dim.1), Dim.2 = mean(Dim.2))

    # Custom cloud plot using ggplot2 following aesthetic_politics style
    p_hcpc_map <- ggplot(mfa.dat, aes(x = Dim.1, y = Dim.2, color = ClusterName)) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
        geom_point(data = centroids_plot, aes(x = Dim.1, y = Dim.2), size = 6, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 2) +
        scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Taste Subculture") +
        labs(
            title = "Culinary Taste Subcultures",
            x = "Fancy Taste Axis (Dim 1)",
            y = "Common Taste Axis (Dim 2)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
            text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            legend.position = "bottom"
        ) +
        guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))
    
    ggsave(here("Plots", "hcpc_map.png"), p_hcpc_map, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

## Profiling the Clusters using Multinomial Logit Analysis
```{r Multinomial Logit}
    # Fit the Multinomial Logit Model predicting cluster membership from demographics
    multinom_model <- multinom(
        Cluster ~ spol.f + epol.f + gend.f + educ + age, 
        data = mfa.dat, 
        trace = FALSE
    )
    
    # Save multinomial logit table as HTML
    tab_model(multinom_model, file = here("Tabs", "multinom_table.html"))
    
    # Calculate predicted probabilities across age and social political ideology
    pred_res <- ggpredict(multinom_model, terms = c("age [all]", "spol.f"))
    pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_res)
    
    # Plot predicted probabilities faceted by cluster
    p_multinom <- ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, fill = group)) +
        geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
        facet_wrap(~response.level, labeller = labeller(response.level = function(x) paste("Cluster", x))) +
        scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Social Ideology") +
        scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Social Ideology") +
        labs(
            title = "Predicted Probabilities of Cluster Membership",
            subtitle = "by Age and Social Ideology",
            x = "Age",
            y = "Predicted Probability"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
            text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
            legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    
    # Save the faceted probability plot
    ggsave(here("Plots", "pred_prob_faceted.png"), p_multinom, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```
